rdf:Description.dcterms:title: Hazard Types
rdf:Description.skos:definition: The Hazard Type vocabulary is a SKOS concept scheme for describing the nature of hazards associated with specific materials, with coverage spanning the biological and geological collections domains.
rdf:Description.dcterms:description: The Hazard Type vocabulary is a SKOS concept scheme for describing the nature of hazards associated with specific materials, with coverage spanning the biological and geological collections domains.
rdf:Description.dcterms:creator: TDWG Mineralogy Extension Task Group
rdf:Description.dcterms:provenance: Compiled by the TDWG Mineralogy Extension Task Group to support community use and the new term hazardType, released as part of a broader set of terms extending Darwin Core coverage to mineralogical and geological collections.
rdf:Description.skos:scopeNote: Despite being published by the Mineralogy Extension Task Group, the hazard type concept scheme is intended to support specimens across that belong to biological and geological collections.
rdf:Description.http:: Intended to be used in conjuncture with the proposed darwin core term, hazardType
rdf:Description.sdo:citation: Norton B, Walcott R, Burri T, Puschnig A, Dombrowski A, Greber N, Hints O, Mansur A, Ralph J, Richard S, Tacker C. Hazard Type Vocabulary (pending).
